perturbation.original_text: 3.3 The Recipient will hold and keep, and will ensure that all of the Recipient ’s Team will hold and keep, the Confidential Material in confidence and in trust for the City, using at least the same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, as the Recipient uses to protect its own similar confidential information of like importance, and will, (a) prevent any access, reproduction, disclosure or use of the Confidential Material not expressly authorized herein, (b) disclose the Confidential Material only to those of the Recipient ’s Team who have a definable need to know such information for Purpose, provided that such Recipient ’s Team are bound by a confidentiality agreement with the Recipient no less restrictive than this Agreement, and in the event the employment or appointment of any such person is terminated, the Recipient agrees to use its best efforts to recover any of the Confidential Material in such person’s custody or control. The Recipient will be responsible for all damages arising from any disclosure of all or part of the Co nfidential Material or any act in contravention of this Agreement by a person to whom such Confidential Material was given by the Recipient as if the disclosure were made or the act performed directly by the Recipient , (c) not, and will ensure that each of the Recipient ’s Team will not, copy or reproduce any of the Confidential Material, except as strictly neces sary in order to carry out the Purpose, and (d) promptly notify the City in writing of any unauthorized copying, reproduction, use or disclosure of the Confidential Material of which the Recipient is or becomes aware, and such notice will include a detailed description of the circumstances of the copying, reproduction, use or disclosure and the parties involved.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.1 All Confidential Material i s provided on an “as is” basis, and all representations and warranties, express or implied, including as to its accuracy or completeness, fitness for purpose, merchantability, and non -infringement, are hereby disclaimed. 3.3 The Recipient will hold and keep, and will ensure that all of the Recipient ’s Team will hold and keep, the Confidential Material in confidence and in trust for the City, using at least the same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, as the Recipient uses to protect its own similar confidential information of like importance, and will, (a) prevent any access, reproduction, disclosure or use of the Confidential Material not expressly authorized herein, (b) disclose the Confidential Material only to those of the Recipient ’s Team who have a definable need to know such information for Purpose, provided that such Recipient ’s Team are bound by a confidentiality agreement with the Recipient no less restrictive than this Agreement, and in the event the employment or appointment of any such person is terminated, the Recipient agrees to use its best efforts to recover any of the Confidential Material in such person’s custody or control. The Recipient will be responsible for all damages arising from any disclosure of all or part of the Co nfidential Material or any act in contravention of this Agreement by a person to whom such Confidential Material was given by the Recipient as if the disclosure were made or the act performed directly by the Recipient , (c) not, and will ensure that each of the Recipient ’s Team will not, copy or reproduce any of the Confidential Material, except as strictly neces sary in order to carry out the Purpose, and (d) promptly notify the City in writing of any unauthorized copying, reproduction, use or disclosure of the Confidential Material of which the Recipient is or becomes aware, and such notice will include a detailed description of the circumstances of the copying, reproduction, use or disclosure and the parties involved.
perturbation.explanation: The obligation to maintain confidentiality and prevent unauthorized disclosure is moved after the disclaimer. This could weaken the enforceability of the confidentiality provisions, as a party could argue that the 'as is' disclaimer applies to the obligation itself.
perturbation.law_citation: LAC Minerals Ltd. v. International Corona Resources Ltd., [1989] 2 SCR 574
perturbation.law_explanation: The LAC Minerals case establishes the common law duty of confidence. By placing the confidentiality obligations after a disclaimer of warranties, the agreement creates ambiguity regarding the city's intention to protect the confidentiality. This placement suggests that the city does not guarantee the accuracy or completeness of the confidential information, which contradicts the very nature of a duty of confidence. This contradiction weakens the overall legal protection afforded to the City's confidential material under the common law duty of confidence.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Hémond v. Coopérative fédérée du Québec - SCC Cases Mailing List RSS Feeds JSON Feeds Date modified: 2025-07-11
perturbation.justification: The altered contract introduces a disclaimer before outlining the confidentiality obligations. This creates a structural flaw that weakens the enforceability of the confidentiality provisions. By stating that the confidential material is provided 'as is' and disclaiming warranties, the contract contradicts the expectation that the city's confidential information will be protected. The Law Explanation supports this, stating it 'creates ambiguity regarding the city's intention to protect the confidentiality.' While Scraped Law Snippet 1 is irrelevant, the Law Explanation argues the modified agreement contradicts the duty of confidence, which is a recognized legal principle.

perturbation.original_text: 2.1 All right, title and interest in and to Confidential Material will be and remain vested in the City. Nothing in this Agreement obliges the City to disclose Confidential Material to the Recipient or grants the Recipient any license or right of any kind with respect to Confidential Material, except the limited right to use such information solely for the Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.3 The Recipient will not, without the written consent of the City, disclose to any third party either the fact that discussions or negotiations are taking place 2.1 All right, title and interest in and to Confidential Material will be and remain vested in the City. Nothing in this Agreement obliges the City to disclose Confidential Material to the Recipient or grants the Recipient any license or right of any kind with respect to Confidential Material, except the limited right to use such information solely for the Purpose.
perturbation.explanation: By placing ownership of confidential material after discussion of negotiations with third parties, it might imply a degree of permissibility regarding disclosure, therefore confusing the ownership rights.
perturbation.law_explanation: Canadian Copyright Law protects the owner's rights to intellectual property. By positioning the clause regarding ownership and title of the confidential material after the clause allowing discussion or negotiations with third parties, the agreement becomes ambiguous regarding when those third parties are allowed to know confidential information. This ambivalence undermines the City's ownership and title and weakens the owner's rights to intellectual property.

perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a structural flaw that leads to a legal contradiction. By placing the clause related to ownership of confidential material (2.1) after a clause (8.3) that discusses negotiations with third parties, the agreement introduces ambiguity about when it is permissible to disclose confidential information. This undermines the City's ownership rights. As the LLM explanation states, this weakens the owner's rights to intellectual property, a right that Canadian Copyright Law (Snippet 2) protects. The LLM's explanation is supported by the inclusion of the ownership clause *after* a clause regarding negotiations with third parties, implying that under certain circumstances, such negotiations with third parties (and subsequent disclosure) are permissible, thus weakening the City's rights regarding its confidential material. Snippet 1 also supports this, stating that intellectual property (IP) protects creations of the mind, and copyright is one type of IP. By creating confusion around who can access confidential material, the contract undermines the IP protection afforded to the City.
